lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The admin corridor at this hour is a different kind of quiet — not the quiet of the wards, which is never truly quiet, but the quiet of institutional authority suspended between decisions. Offices closed. Fluorescent strips doing their work without witness.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Harte's phone rings at six forty-seven.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He picks it up on the second ring. Whoever is calling, he already knows why. You can hear it in the slight accommodation a man makes before he speaks — the breath that comes when you have been expecting bad news long enough that it no longer qualifies as surprising.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The call takes four minutes. He says very little. Three times he says yes. Once he says understood. At no point does he clarify what he means by understood.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He sets the phone face-down on the desk.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The corridor outside his office is empty. The kind of empty that happens just after a decision that no one has been told about yet. An acquisition failing is not like a patient dying — there is no moment of it, no clear before and after. It dissolves. The paperwork ceases to move. The calls stop. The people who were going to benefit from it quietly stop mentioning it in hallways.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Harte remains with the phone face-down for a moment that is longer than it appears from outside.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He picks up a pen. Sets it down. Studies the surface of his desk the way a man studies something he no longer has to decide about. There is a particular stillness that follows: not grief, not relief — something more administrative than either.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The building does not change. The ER below him continues its shift. The bays that were going to be cut remain uncut, at least for now — not because anyone reconsidered them, but because the mechanism that required them has stopped. Somewhere on the next three floors, a ward runs short-staffed. Somewhere else, a trolley waits.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: What has changed is that Harte will not need to defend it.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He stands. Straightens his jacket. Picks up the phone from the desk and puts it in his pocket.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The corridor outside his office receives him the way corridors always receive him — without comment, without ceremony. He walks toward the east wing. There is no announcement. There was never going to be an announcement. The building absorbs it — completely, indifferently, without rest.
